data.change_summary: Extended the minimal-flow remediation beyond the already-added preset wiring by fixing shared flow validation, daemon label provisioning, and user-facing invalid-flow guidance. Requirements drafting now validates recommended flows against `FlowPreset::all()`, GitHub daemon label vocabulary now includes `rb:flow:minimal`, invalid-flow errors derive the supported preset list from the shared enum, flow discovery/run-start conformance coverage now iterates built-in flows, and new unit/CLI coverage verifies built-in flow acceptance, label coverage, discovery output, invalid-flow guidance, and end-to-end minimal-flow execution without `review` or `apply_fixes` stages.
outstanding_risks: Full repository-wide test and conformance suites were not rerun; validation was limited to targeted unit and CLI coverage around the minimal-flow changes.

steps.description: Inspected the current minimal-flow implementation and identified remaining gaps in requirements contracts, daemon label vocabulary, invalid-flow messaging, and stale discovery/startup coverage.
Updated shared flow helpers and error messaging so supported preset guidance comes from the canonical `FlowPreset` list.
Patched requirements-drafting validation and daemon label vocabulary to accept and provision the `minimal` preset consistently.
Expanded CLI, unit, and conformance-facing tests to cover built-in flow discovery, label coverage, and end-to-end minimal-flow execution.
